votes.rationale: The evidence command works correctly across all tested paths: default 2-file scope, narrow --files selection, and all four error cases (disallowed file, missing motion, missing evidence file, nonexistent motion). Evidence is source-fed from the operator file and placed by the model. Atomic write behavior confirmed. Structural governance files and motion.yaml are never touched.

Evidence insertion is correctly narrower than revise: smaller default scope, no motion.yaml access, evidence-file-based input only. The non-invention boundary is enforced in the system prompt with operational language (may quote/organize/summarize, may not upgrade ambiguity or invent conclusions). Human review is the second line of defense. All-or-nothing write semantics confirmed.

Motion Factory v0 now supports the complete authoring cycle: context, draft, revise, and evidence insertion. Each command is progressively narrower in scope and write authority. The evidence command correctly separates evidence placement from judgment. The factory remains a bounded drafting and evidence-placement assistant with no governance authority.
